msg: warning: Variable ch is used uninitialized!
reasoning: Line 29 is 'ch = malloc(sizeof(*ch))'. The tool flags ch as uninitialized at the '*ch' inside sizeof(*ch). However, sizeof is a compile-time operator that does not evaluate its operand — it only uses the type of *ch (i.e., ethtable_t) to compute a size. The pointer value of ch is never actually read. This is the same sizeof false positive pattern as FAMILY_36. The real vbdb bug in bc0ffc0e971 (marked '// ERROR' at line 43) is a use-after-free: in the cleanup loop under ENABLE_FEATURE_CLEAN_UP, delete_eth_table(ch) frees ch and ch->ifname, then the for-loop accesses ch->next on the already-freed memory. The uninitialized_value claim for sizeof(*ch) is a false positive.
